title: What is a digital poster assignment?
plain_text: Digital posters are powerful tools that allow students to be experts and summarize and share what they've learned. When planning a digital poster session, it is essential to decide whether it will be asynchronous or synchronous (virtual). An asynchronous poster session involves pre-recorded presentations, which can be accessed at any time by the students and other audience members. Synchronous poster sessions involve real-time scheduled virtual presentations where the presenters and audience members are present simultaneously. Table of Contents Digital posters: asynchronous vs. synchronous Digital presentation tools Digital tools for asynchronous format Digital tools for synchronous format Assignment design and delivery options Virtual poster sessions Lightning Talks Student resources Technical support Digital posters: asynchronous vs. synchronous Both asynchronous and synchronous poster presentation formats have advantages and disadvantages, many of which are compared below. Digital presentation tools Once you have selected the poster presentation format (asynchronous or synchronous), explore the digital tools available for that given format. While there are a plethora of digital tool options to choose from, the table below compares the asynchronous and synchronous poster session formats available to you at Northeastern University. This section will outline our suggested tools for asynchronous and synchronous poster sessions and considerations for each. Digital tools for asynchronous format Digital tools for an asynchronous format: Canvas Studio, VoiceThread, Lucidchart, Miro Interactive Whiteboards. Digital tools for synchronous format Digital tools for synchronous format: Lucid for Education suite, Miro, Teams Meetings, Zoom for Teaching and Learning. Assignment design and delivery options Digital poster discussions effectively include self-identified introverts and underrepresented students in the conversation. Leverage digital poster assignments or video Lightning Talks to foster inclusive activities, develop communication skills, and support undergraduate peer mentor interactions with students. Virtual poster sessions VoiceThread gallery assignments with peer review. Refer to How do I create a VoiceThread presentation gallery assignment? for more instructions. Synchronous poster sessions with Zoom. Zoom's breakout rooms feature allows participants to present and discuss in small groups. Lucidspark or Miro Interactive Whiteboards. Use the Lucidspark gallery walk template or the Miro Poster session template to host an asynchronous poster session or combine it with Zoom for a synchronous session. Lightning Talks Lightning Talks offers an alternative to the traditional poster format. Students create, share, and comment on 3-5 min Lightning Talks (Ignite) supported by a series of PowerPoint slides. Student resources Once you have selected the format you want to use, consider sharing relevant resources with students: Tips on designing conference posters, How to participate in a virtual poster session, How do I submit a VoiceThread assignment as a student?, How do I create and submit a group VoiceThread presentation as a student?, How do I embed a Miro interactive whiteboard directly into a Canvas page?, How do I submit an assignment in Canvas using Lucid? Technical support Email athelp@northeastern.edu, Book a consultation with an Instructional Design Consultant, Miro Help Center, Getting Started with Miro - Instructor-facilitated training.
